name: Raisin Crisscross Pie- Better Homes and Gardens
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, combine brown sugar and cornstarch. Stir in raisins, orange peel, orange juice, lemon peel, lemon juice, and water.
2. Cook over medium heat until thick and bubbly. Continue stirring for an additional minute.
3. Remove from heat and stir in walnuts.
4. Fill a pastry-lined 9-inch pie plate with the raisin mixture.
5. Adjust the lattice crust by fluting the edge.
6. Cover the edge of the pie with foil.
7. Bake at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for 20 minutes.
8. Remove foil and bake an additional 20 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown.
Pastry for Lattice Top Pie:
1. In a mixing bowl, stir together flour and salt.
2. Cut in shortening until pieces are the size of small peas.
3. Sprinkle 1 tablespoon of water over part of the mixture, gently tossing with a fork.
4. Push the moistened part to one side of the bowl.
5. Repeat with the remaining dry ingredients.
6. Form dough into a ball. Divide in half.
7. Flatten one half on a lightly floured surface and roll out to a circle about 12 inches in diameter.
8. Unroll the pastry onto a 9-inch pie plate, being careful not to stretch it.
9. Trim the pastry 1/2 inch beyond the edge of the pie.
10. Roll out the other half of the pastry into strips, approximately 1/2 inch wide.
11. Weave the strips on top of the filling to make a lattice crust.
12. Press the ends of the strips into the rim of the crust.
13. Fold the bottom pastry over the lattice strips and flute.
14. Bake as directed in the individual recipe.
